title: Fish Cakes
excerpt: Today is the day for fish cakes. Not difficult to make at all and always a winner. INGREDIENTS 500 g cooked fish 300 g cooked potatoes 1 cup (250ml) finely chopped parsley 1 onion, grated 2 eggs salt pepper 30 ml lemon juice bought breadcrumbs to roll them in 1 …
html:
Today is the day for fish cakes. Not difficult to make at all and always a winner.

INGREDIENTS

500 g cooked fish

300 g cooked potatoes

1 cup (250ml) finely chopped parsley

1 onion, grated

2 eggs

salt

pepper

30 ml lemon juice

bought breadcrumbs to roll them in

1 bottle of oil to fry them in.

METHOD

1. Cook the fish in water that just covers it for 20 minutes. The fish flakes easily when you press on it with a fork.

2. Peel the potatoes and cut them into quarters. Cook them in water until soft. If a fork goes in easily, it's soft. Mash it with a fork or a potato masher. Mash it.

3. Chop the parsley finely. Put it in a mug and snip it with scissors in the mug. That cuts it nice and fine.

4. Peel the onion and grate it finely with your grater or chop it finely in your food processor.

5. Whisk the eggs with a fork until well mixed. Just whisk them in a mug.

6. Place the fish, mashed potatoes, parsley, onion, salt, pepper, onions and lemon juice in a bowl. Mix everything nicely with your hands. Make cakes about the size of golf balls and press them a little flat.

7. Roll the fish cakes in the breadcrumbs. Place them on a plate and let them rest for 1 hour in the fridge.

8. Put the oil in a deep pan and let it get hot. Fry the fish cakes to a nice brown in the oil.

 

Enjoy.
